should_trigger: Design an auto-orchestrator gate that decides whether to accept an LLM answer, call a verifier, or escalate to a human while controlling false accepts.
We need a routing policy that chooses among three specialist agents from noisy retrieval scores, model confidences, and prior success rates. Use detection theory.
Create a sequential test for whether a tool result is consistent with the plan, with an option to gather another observation before retrying.
Our orchestrator has a confidence score but no explicit threshold. Convert it into a Bayes or Neyman-Pearson decision rule with measurable error rates.
Estimate task difficulty and tool reliability over time, then use those estimates to decide whether to run a costly second model.
should_not_trigger: Summarize the history of statistical signal processing for a nontechnical blog post.
Implement a deterministic YAML parser for orchestrator configuration files.
Pick a CSS color palette for the dashboard.
edge_cases: We have no labeled outcomes or likelihood model yet, but need a provisional safety threshold for a high-volume orchestrator route. Design the temporary rule and instrumentation.
